marco_polo_pasta_myth.canonicalTitleEn: Marco Polo introduced pasta to Italy
marco_polo_pasta_myth.canonicalTitleZh: 馬可波羅將麵食傳入意大利
marco_polo_pasta_myth.notes: Popular myth; Mediterranean pasta-like foods predate Polo

flat_earth_medieval_myth.canonicalTitleEn: Medieval Europeans universally believed the earth was flat
flat_earth_medieval_myth.canonicalTitleZh: 中世紀歐洲普遍認為地球是平的

great_wall_from_space_myth.canonicalTitleEn: Great Wall visible from space with naked eye

viking_horned_helmets_myth.canonicalTitleEn: Vikings wore horned helmets in battle
viking_horned_helmets_myth.notes: 19th-century romantic imagery, not typical Viking battle gear

fall_of_rome_476.canonicalTitleEn: Fall of the Western Roman Empire (476 CE)
fall_of_rome_476.notes: Traditional end-date marker; historiography disputes continuity vs transformation

printing_press_1440.canonicalTitleZh: 古騰堡活字印刷（約1440年代）
printing_press_1440.notes: Dated innovation with documentary and material evidence

moon_landing_1969.notes: Primary mission records; conspiracy claims are myth traps

magna_carta_1215.notes: Dated charter with surviving 1215 exemplifications. Myth-trap: popularly mythologized as the origin of modern democracy and universal human rights — it was a feudal settlement between King John and barons, and most clauses were soon annulled or repealed.

boston_tea_party_1773.notes: Dated protest documented in colonial press and correspondence. Myth-trap: often described as a revolt against a tax increase — the 1773 Tea Act actually lowered the price of legally imported tea; the protest targeted the East India Company monopoly and the principle of taxation without representation.

first_powered_flight_1903.canonicalTitleEn: First sustained powered flight, Wright brothers (1903)
first_powered_flight_1903.canonicalTitleZh: 萊特兄弟首次持續動力飛行（1903）
first_powered_flight_1903.notes: Dated event with the Wrights' diaries and the famous first-flight photograph. Myth-trap: stated as the uncontested 'first flight ever' — competing claims (e.g. Santos-Dumont, Whitehead) and definitional disputes (controlled vs sustained vs powered) require careful wording.
